retry_reason: If the lookups fail, the ACPI device does not match any SPI controller; retrying with the same inputs will not change the outcome unless device enumeration changes, which is typically not expected in this context.
contract_summary: This function looks up an SPI controller by ACPI device, acquiring a reference to the underlying device via class_find_device. On success, it returns a pointer to the spi_controller with its device's reference count incremented. The caller must ensure the reference is released by calling put_device on the spi_controller's dev member (or via a suitable put function) when the controller is no longer needed. Returns NULL if no matching device is found; in that case, no reference is acquired.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to locate and return an spi_controller, acquiring a reference on its embedded device via class_find_device. No other significant logic; the function is a thin wrapper around device class lookup and container_of.
judge.retry_reason: If the lookups fail, the ACPI device does not match any SPI controller; retrying with the same inputs will not change the outcome unless device enumeration changes, which is typically not expected in this context.

check.reason: class_find_device() increments the refcount of the found device. The function returns the corresponding spi_controller via container_of. The access path controller.dev.kobj.kref.refcount correctly traces from the returned spi_controller through its embedded device, kobject, kref, to refcount_t. The type chain matches. The operation direction (get) and location (return) are correct.
